steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content: The passage lists impressive accomplishments—Broadway, touring with Prince, President's Council—then states “nothing in her career matches” a specific honor. The word “nothing” signals a reversal: despite all these achievements, one honor stands supreme.

steps.title: Choose the transition that signals contrast
steps.content:
“However” introduces the necessary contrast, pivoting from accomplishments to the claim that one honor surpasses them all.

Thus would suggest logical consequence; For example would introduce another achievement; Second of all would continue a list.

The passage requires a word that acknowledges the achievements while presenting this honor as distinct and supreme—exactly what “However” provides.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
